Title: Enhancing iron content and growth of cucumber seedlings with MgFe-LDHs under low-temperature stress

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated by MgFe-LDH treatment as part of the peroxidase response associated with improved cold tolerance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. This peroxidase-related gene was among the genes selected for qRT-PCR validation after transcriptome analysis. 2. MgFe-LDH treatment increased expression of peroxidase genes and overall POD activity. 3. The induced peroxidase response was linked to enhanced stress tolerance under low temperature.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root tissues after 10 days of LDH treatment under low-temperature stress.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA1083769; qRT-PCR validation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific biochemical substrate and exact peroxidase isoform function were not described in the text.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Peroxidase genes may serve as markers or auxiliary targets for improving cold-stress resilience together with nutrient-enhancing treatments.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated by MgFe-LDH treatment as part of the enhanced peroxidase defense system.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. This gene was included among peroxidase-related genes validated after transcriptome analysis. 2. MgFe-LDH treatment elevated expression of peroxidase genes in roots. 3. Higher peroxidase expression coincided with increased POD activity and improved cold resistance.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA1083769; qRT-PCR validation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper did not map this accession to a gene symbol or specific peroxidase isoenzyme name.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a stress-response biomarker under Fe-enhancing nanofertilizer treatment.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression increased after MgFe-LDH treatment, supporting enhanced ROS-scavenging capacity and cold tolerance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. This accession was among genes tested by qRT-PCR following transcriptomic identification of strong peroxidase induction. 2. LDH treatment increased peroxidase-gene expression and POD activity. 3. The induced peroxidase system was proposed to help alleviate oxidative stress under chilling conditions.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA1083769; qRT-PCR validation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact enzyme isoform specificity was not given.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker gene for nanofertilizer-induced stress adaptation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated under LDH treatment, consistent with higher POD activity and better chilling tolerance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified among peroxidase-related genes examined by qRT-PCR. 2. LDH induced peroxidase transcript accumulation in roots. 3. Increased peroxidase activity was associated with improved low-temperature stress resistance.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA1083769; qRT-PCR validation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes peroxidase gene class rather than individual molecular function.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential biomarker for selecting treatments or genotypes with stronger oxidative stress protection.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by MgFe-LDH treatment as part of the peroxidase-associated cold response.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. This gene was one of the peroxidase-related accessions analyzed by qRT-PCR. 2. MgFe-LDH increased peroxidase-gene expression in cucumber roots. 3. Peroxidase induction supported the interpretation that LDH enhances stress resistance through ROS-related defenses.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA1083769; qRT-PCR validation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct metabolite product assigned; function interpreted at stress-defense level.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful in stress-tolerance screening and mechanistic validation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression was elevated by MgFe-LDH treatment in association with increased peroxidase activity and improved seedling performance under cold stress.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. This accession was included in qRT-PCR validation of transcriptomic results. 2. LDH enhanced the expression of peroxidase-associated genes and increased POD activity. 3. The peroxidase response was proposed to contribute to reduced oxidative damage and stronger cold tolerance.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA1083769; qRT-PCR validation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific annotation beyond accession number was not provided in the paper text.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker gene for evaluating nanofertilizer-induced antioxidant defense.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Fatty acid desaturase family; membrane-bound omega-3 desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fatty acid desaturation / alpha-linolenic acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Polyunsaturated fatty acid unsaturation in roots under cold response
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: alpha-linolenic acid-containing lipids / more unsaturated fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces an additional double bond into omega-6 unsaturated fatty acids to form omega-3 unsaturated fatty acids, increasing membrane lipid unsaturation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; presented as a critical expressed FAD gene in cucumber roots/hypocotyls under low-temperature stress.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances cell membrane stability and cold tolerance under low-temperature stress.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root tissues / cell membranes of cucumber seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MgFe-LDH treatment strongly upregulated CsFAD3 expression (up to ~7-fold), associated with enhanced cold tolerance and seedling growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; hormone measurement (SA); phenotyping under low-temperature stress; transcript-supported inference
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsFAD3 was one of only two FAD genes reported as significantly expressed in cucumber roots and hypocotyls. 2. MgFe-LDH treatment increased SA content and upregulated CsFAD3 expression by up to 7-fold, whereas CsFAD2.1 remained largely unchanged. 3. The study concludes that increased CsFAD3 expression raises fatty acid unsaturation and contributes to improved cold resistance and seedling growth.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly expressed in roots and hypocotyls under low-temperature stress.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root tissues after 10 days of LDH treatment under low-temperature stress.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA1083769; qRT-PCR validation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The mechanistic link to Fe status is hypothesized but not directly validated; SA-mediated induction is inferred from prior literature and observed correlation in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving cucumber cold tolerance and seedling establishment by engineering higher root/hypocotyl CsFAD3 activity or combining with Fe-enhancing nanofertilizer strategies.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Fatty acid desaturase family; membrane-bound omega-6 desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a double bond into oleic acid-containing lipids to generate omega-6 unsaturated fatty acids.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to membrane lipid unsaturation linked to stress adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression remained relatively unchanged after MgFe-LDH treatment and was not implicated as the main responsive gene.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsFAD2.1, together with CsFAD3, was identified as one of the two FAD genes significantly expressed in cucumber roots and hypocotyls. 2. Unlike CsFAD3, CsFAD2.1 expression remained relatively unchanged after LDH treatment. 3. It serves as a contextual core tested gene but was not the main driver of the observed cold-tolerance effect.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly expressed in roots and hypocotyls.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root tissues after 10 days of treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; study context supported by RNA-seq PRJNA1083769
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was directly assayed in Results, but evidence does not support it as the causal main responder for nutrient-metabolism-linked cold tolerance in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority than CsFAD3 for engineering based on this study.
